creator: amdmi3@mail.ru
text:
* Pav Lucistnik (pav@FreeBSD.org) wrote:
> Can't reproduce. This looks like some else event.h header got
> included instead of the once from ./include
> 
> Can you check your system for event.h files, especially in
> /usr/include and in /usr/local/include ?

You're right, I have /usr/local/include/event.h which belongs to
/usr/ports/devel/libevent. I've made a patch which fixes this issue
(daimonin internal include dirs are now passed to CC before system
ones). I'll also sent this patch to authors.

--- daimonin-client.patch begins here ---
diff -ruN daimonin-client.orig/files/patch-src-Makefile.in daimonin-client/files/patch-src-Makefile.in
--- daimonin-client.orig/files/patch-src-Makefile.in	Thu Jan  1 03:00:00 1970
+++ daimonin-client/files/patch-src-Makefile.in	Sun Sep  3 20:17:25 2006
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+--- ../../src/Makefile.in.orig	Sat Aug  5 00:06:02 2006
++++ ../../src/Makefile.in	Sun Sep  3 20:16:50 2006
+@@ -96,6 +96,6 @@
+ 	$(RM) -f Makefile ./include/define.h
+ 
+ .c.o:
+-	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(INCLUDES) -c $*.c
++	$(CC) $(INCLUDES) $(CFLAGS) -c $*.c
+ 
+ all: daimonin
--- daimonin-client.patch ends here ---

-- 
Best regards,
 Dmitry                          mailto:amdmi3@mail.ru
